campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies in the city have to pay. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council publicly discussed and debated the matter before making a decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases for fiscal year 2026
Finance department recommendations on tax adjustments
Public council deliberation and voting on the ordinance
Municipal code amendments affecting local businesses

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, they moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the standard first reading process to speed things up, with plans to finalize the vote at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next council meeting
Expedited approval process used for this item

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction services and professional agreements to move forward with town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved financial reports for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved town improvement projects
Authorized three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to plan out their upcoming agendas for May 5th and May 12th, 2026. They reviewed which items to add to each agenda, decided if any topics needed specific time slots, and checked documents to make sure proper public notice requirements were met.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 5, 2026 meeting agenda
Reviewed draft items for the May 12, 2026 meeting
Assigned specific time slots to certain agenda topics
Verified proper public notice procedures were followed
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and heard from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding on neighborhood grant requests for the 2026-27 budget year, determining which community projects would receive city funding.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 fiscal year
Public comment period for residents
Community project funding recommendations
Council committee reports and staff updates

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting focused on interviewing candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were given opportunities to provide public comments both in person and online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online)
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 30, 2026 to approve meeting notes from the Economic Development Committee's January and March sessions. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any issues they wanted to bring up, though speakers were limited to 3 minutes each.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for residents on committee-related matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss putting up large promotional signs on buildings around Levi's Stadium for the FIFA World Cup 2026 events. They also worked on finalizing a new employment agreement with the city's firefighters that will run through 2029.
keyTopics: Approval of temporary large signs/billboards near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters
One agenda item was withdrawn by city staff before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to discuss several financial and development matters. They held public hearings and voted on approving the city's budget for the next fiscal year (2026-27), reissuing a tax responsibility act, and updating various user fees and charges. The council also heard a presentation about Santa Clara County's plan to address homelessness in the area.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget and development spending approval
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
Updates to city user fees and service charges
County homelessness prevention and support plan
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss routine administrative matters and consider a major development proposal for Old Trace Road. The main item was a request to subdivide a vacant 1-acre lot into 9 residential lots to build 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller secondary homes on the same properties).
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1.02-acre lot on Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots
Proposed construction of 9 single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Environmental review completed under state law (CEQA exempt)
Election of council chair and vice chair
Zoning classification: Residential Estate district
